title: hieracium racemosum subsp. caramanicum
intro: Hieracium racemosum subsp. caramanicum, also known as Hieracium caramanicum, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant in the dandelion genus, native to certain regions. It belongs to the Asteraceae family and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are believed to offer health benefits such as wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. Hieracium racemosum subsp. caramanicum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in homeopathic remedies.
